mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is still rising, markets infer prolonged accommodation and stronger nominal growth later; that combination lifts inflation expectations and term premia, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS stays near zero and CPIAUCSL continues to accelerate, but the 10-year yield falls or is flat in a majority of 20-day windows over the next year.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or larger rise in UNRATE over roughly three months signals deteriorating labor demand, stronger recession pricing, and a flight-to-quality bid for Treasuries, which lowers the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp in 3 months, the 10-year yield rises in more than 60% of triggered 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both accelerate together, the market reads broad nominal demand strength as reducing recession odds and raising expected future rates, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not coincide with positive 20-day yield changes more often than 50% of the time in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing rebound, a sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger real activity and less slack; investors then price higher future policy normalization and inflation compensation, which lifts the 10-year yield over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO moves above its 6-month moving average but the 10-year yield fails to trend higher over the following 60 trading days in most cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp housing starts collapse signals weaker household demand and a faster growth slowdown than other monthly indicators, causing Treasury buying and a lower 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops at least 10% below its 3-month average yet the 10-year yield rises or remains unchanged in the subsequent 20-day window more than half the time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains far below CPI inflation, real policy rates are deeply negative and the market expects continued easing pressure; this lifts expected inflation and term premium, raising the 10-year Treasury yield over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS spread exceeds 2 percentage points but the 10-year yield does not rise over the following 5 trading days in a majority of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS confirms a demand-side slowdown that compresses expected growth and inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous UNRATE increases and PAYEMS declines do not correspond to negative 20-day yield changes more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a broad-based demand shock regime, weak retail sales together with weak GDP growth signal a sustained nominal growth downshift; that lowers inflation expectations and supports Treasury duration, pushing the 10-year yield down over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in RSAFS and GDPC1 is followed by flat or rising 10-year yields over 60 trading days in more than half of observed episodes.
